text:
First, note that Europe/London in 1140 would have been using local solar time (no standardized time zones). However, for consistency with the category rules, we assume the question intends modern time zone rules applied historically (which is anachronistic but typical for such exercises).  

Europe/London is normally UTC+0 (or UTC+1 in summer), but in 1140, daylight saving time did not exist.  
Thus, Europe/London = UTC+0.  

2 PM UTC = 14:00 UTC → same in London: 14:00.  

Date remains November 27, 1140.  

FINAL_ANSWER: 1140-11-27 14:00
